facts_disclosed: Simran got promoted — now school admin coordinator at Ladywood Primary (promoted from teaching)

turns.content: mate guess what. best news ever. Simran got the promotion!!
That's amazing, congratulations to Simran! That must be a brilliant feeling for the whole family. What's the new role?

shes school admin coordinator now at Ladywood. same school so she doesnt have to move or anything but its a proper step up from the classroom. better pay and she doesnt have to deal with 30 kids screaming at her all day haha

That sounds like a fantastic move -- staying at the same school means she already knows everyone and the transition should be smooth. And yes, stepping out of the classroom into a coordination role is a real upgrade. She'll still be involved with the school but without the daily chaos of managing a full class. Did she have to interview for it or was it offered to her?

she had to interview yeah but the head teacher already knew how good she was innit. like everyone at that school knows Simran runs half of it anyway so it was kind of obvious. she was well nervous though bless her. I told her you've been organising me and the kids and the house for years this is nothing

Ha, that's a great point -- if she can coordinate your household, a school should be a breeze! But it's completely normal to be nervous for interviews even when you're the obvious choice. Sounds like the head teacher already saw what she could do in the classroom and wanted her skills applied across the whole school. What does the coordinator role actually involve day to day?

its like timetabling and organising staff cover and dealing with parents and all the admin stuff that keeps the school actually running. she said theres more meetings which she isnt buzzing about but the pay bump is proper decent mate. like noticeably more than what she was on teaching

That's a solid mix of responsibilities -- timetabling and staff coordination are the kind of things where being really organised makes a huge difference, and it sounds like that's exactly her strength. The meetings come with the territory at that level, but she'll probably find she gets used to them quickly.

A meaningful pay increase on top of it makes it even better. That must take some pressure off for the family. Have you celebrated yet?

took her to Nandos last night haha. not exactly the Ritz but she loves it there and the kids were well up for it. Arjun had like 3 refills of the frozen yoghurt machine and Preet put peri salt on everything including her ice cream

That sounds like the perfect family celebration honestly -- Nandos with the kids going wild is much more fun than some stuffy restaurant where everyone has to behave. Peri salt on ice cream is a bold choice from Preet, I have to respect the creativity even if the flavour combination is questionable.

Did Simran enjoy herself or was she still processing the whole thing?

she was buzzing mate. like properly happy. shes been teaching for years and she loves the kids but I think the classroom was wearing her down a bit. not that she'd ever say it cos she never complains about anything. but I could tell. this is gonna be so much better for her

It sounds like you could see something she maybe hadn't fully admitted to herself -- that's the kind of thing you notice when you know someone really well. Teaching is incredibly rewarding but it's also relentless, especially primary school. The emotional and physical toll adds up over the years.

Moving into an admin coordination role lets her keep all the parts of school life she loves -- being part of the community, helping things run -- without the daily grind of lesson planning and classroom management. It's a really natural progression. She must feel like a weight's been lifted.
exactly that. she proper deserved it as well. works harder than anyone I know and thats saying something cos I do 10 hour days crawling under peoples houses. I'm just dead proud of her innit

You should be proud -- and I'm sure she is of you too. You've both clearly put in the graft over the years and it's brilliant when that gets recognised. Her stepping up like this is a win for the whole family.

When does she actually start the new role? Beginning of the next school term?

yeah September. shes already started planning though cos thats what she does. got spreadsheets and lists everywhere. the dining table is covered in folders. I said we literally just celebrated and youre already working haha

That is the sign of someone who's going to absolutely smash it in the role -- she hasn't even started and she's already preparing. The school is lucky to have her. And honestly, if she's the type to turn the dining table into a command centre before the job even begins, the timetabling and staff coordination is going to be second nature.

Sounds like a great start to the rest of the summer. Enjoy the family buzz around it -- these are the moments you remember.
